seoTitle: How to Add Fonts on Google Docs (2026 Complete Guide)
metaDescription: Add new fonts to Google Docs from the extended web font library, filter by category, and set your favorites as the default style for future documents.
intro: The standard font dropdown in Google Docs only shows a handful of typefaces, but there's a much larger library hiding behind one extra click. This guide covers opening the extended font catalog, filtering by category to find the right style, and saving your favorites as a default so future documents start with your preferred look already applied.

steps.heading: Open the Font dropdown
steps.body: Click the Font family selector on the main toolbar to see your currently available typefaces.

steps.heading: Access the extended font library
steps.body: Select More fonts at the top of the dropdown to launch the full web font catalog with hundreds of additional styles.

steps.heading: Filter by category
steps.body: Use the filter options to browse by Serif, Sans-Serif, Display, Monospace, or Handwriting to narrow down the right match for your document.

steps.heading: Search and add fonts to your toolkit
steps.body: Use the search bar to find specific font names, then click to add them directly to your active font list.

steps.heading: Clean up your font list
steps.body: Remove unused or cluttered styles from your active fonts list to keep the dropdown organized and easy to navigate.

steps.heading: Set your favorite as the default style
steps.body: Save your preferred font as the Default style so it automatically applies to all new documents going forward.

faq.question: Are the extra fonts in the web font library free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, the additional fonts available through More fonts are all free Google Fonts that anyone can add and use.

faq.question: Will fonts I add show up for collaborators too?
faq.answer: Once applied to text in a shared document, the font renders for anyone viewing it, since Google Docs uses the same web font library across accounts.

faq.question: How do I remove a font I no longer want in my dropdown?
faq.answer: Reopen the extended font panel and deselect the font to remove it from your active list without affecting text already using it.

faq.question: Can I set a different default font for each document type?
faq.answer: Default styles apply per document through paragraph styles, so you can set a unique default in each file rather than one global setting.
